checks.shortDescription: A function that uses more functions and variables from another file than functions and variables from its own file.
checks.title: Feature envy

checks.shortDescription: Finds classes that belong to no namespace.
checks.title: Global namespace classes

checks.shortDescription: Two files share too much information about each other (accessing many operations or variables in both directions).
checks.title: Inappropriate Intimacy

checks.shortDescription: Finds classes that depend on many other types; they could probably be refactored into smaller classes with fewer dependencies.
checks.title: Classes with too many source dependencies

checks.shortDescription: Finds classes with many fields; they could probably be refactored by breaking them down into smaller classes, and using composition.
checks.title: Classes with too many fields

checks.shortDescription: Finds functions which call too many other functions.
checks.title: Complex functions

checks.shortDescription: Functions with high cyclomatic complexity.
checks.title: Cyclomatic Complexity

checks.shortDescription: Finds functions with many parameters; they could probably be refactored by wrapping parameters into a struct.
checks.title: Functions with too many parameters

checks.shortDescription: Blocks with too many consecutive statements are candidates for refactoring.
checks.title: Block with too many statements

checks.shortDescription: Boolean expressions that are too deeply nested are hard to read and understand.
checks.title: Complex condition

checks.shortDescription: When there is nothing to rethrow, attempting to rethrow an exception will terminate the program.
checks.title: Accidental rethrow

checks.shortDescription: Catching an exception by value will create a copy of the thrown exception, thereby potentially slicing the original exception object.
checks.title: Catching by value

checks.shortDescription: If an exception is allocated on the heap, then it should be deleted when caught.
checks.title: Leaky catch

checks.shortDescription: Exceptions should be objects rather than pointers to objects.
checks.title: Throwing pointers

checks.shortDescription: NULL-condition guards before function calls to the memory-deallocation function free(3) are unnecessary, because passing NULL to free(3) is a no-op.
checks.title: Guarded Free

checks.shortDescription: A local variable hides a parameter.
checks.title: Declaration hides parameter

checks.shortDescription: A local variable hides another local variable from a surrounding scope.
checks.title: Declaration hides variable

checks.shortDescription: A local variable or parameter that hides a global variable of the same name.
checks.title: Local variable hides global variable

checks.shortDescription: If expressions before and after a comma operator use different indentation, it is easy to misread the purpose of the code.
checks.title: Comma before misleading indentation

checks.shortDescription: An empty block after a conditional can be a sign of an omission and can decrease maintainability of the code.
checks.title: Empty branch of conditional

checks.shortDescription: Accessing an array offset before checking the range means that the program may attempt to read beyond the end of a buffer
checks.title: Array offset used before range check

checks.shortDescription: Assigning a non-reference instance of a derived type to a variable of the base type slices off all members added by the derived class, and can cause an unexpected state.
checks.title: Slicing

checks.shortDescription: Japanese era changes can lead to code behaving differently.
checks.title: Hard-coded Japanese era start date

checks.shortDescription: 'Magic constants' should be avoided: if a nontrivial constant is used repeatedly, it should be encapsulated into a const variable or macro definition.
checks.title: Magic numbers

checks.shortDescription: 'Magic constants' should be avoided: if a nontrivial constant is used repeatedly, it should be encapsulated into a const variable or macro definition.
checks.title: Magic strings

checks.shortDescription: A numeric literal that matches the initializer of a constant variable was found.
checks.title: Magic numbers: use defined constant

checks.shortDescription: A string literal that matches the initializer of a constant variable was found.
checks.title: Magic strings: use defined constant

checks.shortDescription: When public methods can be overridden, base classes are unable to enforce invariants that should hold for the whole hierarchy.
checks.title: Public virtual method

checks.shortDescription: When public methods can be overridden, base classes are unable to enforce invariants that should hold for the whole hierarchy.
checks.title: Public virtual method in Hub Class

checks.shortDescription: Classes that have an explicit destructor, copy constructor, or copy assignment operator may behave inconsistently if they do not have all three.
checks.title: Rule of three

checks.shortDescription: Classes that have an explicit copy constructor or copy assignment operator may behave inconsistently if they do not have both.
checks.title: Inconsistent definition of copy constructor and assignment ('Rule of Two')

checks.shortDescription: Global variables should have descriptive names, to help document their use, avoid namespace pollution and reduce the risk of shadowing with local variables.
checks.title: Short global name

checks.shortDescription: A switch statement with too much code in its cases can make the control flow hard to follow.
checks.title: Long switch case

checks.shortDescription: Finds #include directives that are not needed because none of the included elements are used.
checks.title: Unused include

checks.shortDescription: A local variable that is never called or accessed may be an indication that the code is incomplete or has a typo.
checks.title: Unused local variable

checks.shortDescription: A static function that is never called or accessed may be an indication that the code is incomplete or has a typo.
checks.title: Unused static function

checks.shortDescription: A static variable that is never accessed may be an indication that the code is incomplete or has a typo.
checks.title: Unused static variable

checks.shortDescription: The goto statement can make the control flow of a function hard to understand, when used for purposes other than error handling.
checks.title: Use of goto

checks.shortDescription: The condition of the branching statement always evaluates to the same value.
checks.title: Branching condition always evaluates to same value

checks.shortDescription: Unused functions may increase object size, decrease readability, and create the possibility of misuse.
checks.title: Function is never called

checks.shortDescription: A goto or break statement is followed by unreachable code.
checks.title: Dead code due to goto or break statement

checks.shortDescription: Failing to close resources in the function that opened them makes it difficult to avoid and detect resource leaks.
checks.title: Open descriptor may not be closed

checks.shortDescription: Functions that always return before closing the socket they opened leak resources.
checks.title: Open descriptor never closed

checks.shortDescription: Freeing a resource more than once can lead to undefined behavior and cause memory corruption.
checks.title: Potential double free

checks.shortDescription: A function may return before closing a file that was opened in the function.
checks.title: Open file may not be closed

checks.shortDescription: A function always returns before closing a file that was opened in the function.
checks.title: Open file is not closed

checks.shortDescription: Using an uninitialized variable may lead to undefined results.
checks.title: Global variable may be used before initialization

checks.shortDescription: A dereferenced pointer is not checked for nullness in this location, but it is checked in other locations.
checks.title: Inconsistent null check of pointer

checks.shortDescription: Failing to account for EOF in a call to a scanf-like function can lead to undefined behavior.
checks.title: Incorrect return-value check for a 'scanf'-like function

checks.shortDescription: Not running initialization code may lead to unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Initialization code not run

checks.shortDescription: An object larger than 64 bytes is passed by value to a function.
checks.title: Large object passed by value

checks.shortDescription: Accessing a pointer or array using an offset before checking if the value is positive may result in unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Pointer offset used before it is checked

checks.shortDescription: A function may return before freeing memory that was allocated in the function.
checks.title: Memory may not be freed

checks.shortDescription: A function always returns before freeing memory that was allocated in the function.
checks.title: Memory is never freed

checks.shortDescription: Failing to check that a call to 'scanf' actually writes to an output variable can lead to unexpected behavior at reading time.
checks.title: Missing return-value check for a 'scanf'-like function

checks.shortDescription: Using a return value as a pointer offset without checking that the value is positive may lead to buffer overruns.
checks.title: Unchecked return value used as offset

checks.shortDescription: Dereferencing an untested value from a function that can return null may lead to undefined behavior.
checks.title: Returned pointer not checked

checks.shortDescription: An array allocated with 'new[]' is being freed using 'delete'.
checks.title: 'new[]' array freed with 'delete'

checks.shortDescription: An object that was allocated with 'new' is being freed using 'delete[]'.
checks.title: 'new' object freed with 'delete[]'

checks.shortDescription: An object that was allocated with 'malloc' or 'new' is being freed using a mismatching 'free' or 'delete'.
checks.title: Mismatching new/free or malloc/delete

checks.shortDescription: Using an uninitialized variable may lead to undefined results.
checks.title: Variable not initialized before use

checks.shortDescription: A buffer allocated using 'malloc' may not have enough space for a string that is being copied into it.
checks.title: Buffer not sufficient for string

checks.shortDescription: Calling a copy operation with a size derived from the source buffer instead of the destination buffer may result in a buffer overflow.
checks.title: Copy function using source size

checks.shortDescription: Exceeding the size of a static array during write or access operations may result in a buffer overflow.
checks.title: Static array access may cause overflow

checks.shortDescription: Using a pointer to stack memory after the function has returned gives undefined results.
checks.title: Pointer to stack object used as return value

checks.shortDescription: A call to a function ignores its return value, but at least 90% of the total number of calls to the function check the return value.
checks.title: Return value of a function is ignored

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'malloc', 'calloc' or 'realloc' without allocating enough memory to contain an instance of the type of the pointer may result in a buffer overflow
checks.title: Not enough memory allocated for pointer type

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'malloc', 'calloc' or 'realloc' without allocating enough memory to contain multiple instances of the type of the pointer may result in a buffer overflow
checks.title: Not enough memory allocated for array of pointer type

checks.shortDescription: Assigning a value to a variable that is not used may indicate an error in the code.
checks.title: Variable is assigned a value that is never read

checks.shortDescription: An allocated memory block is used after it has been freed.
checks.title: Potential use after free

checks.shortDescription: Commented-out code makes the remaining code more difficult to read.
checks.title: Commented-out code

checks.shortDescription: Functions used from outside the file they are declared in should be documented, as they are part of a public API.
checks.title: Undocumented API function

checks.shortDescription: Comments containing 'FIXME' indicate that the code has known bugs.
checks.title: FIXME comment

checks.shortDescription: Comments containing 'TODO' indicate that the code may be in an incomplete state.
checks.title: TODO comment

checks.shortDescription: Large functions that have no or almost no comments are likely to be too complex to understand and maintain.
checks.title: Poorly documented large function

checks.shortDescription: Using the same include guard macro in more than one header file may cause unexpected behavior from the compiler.
checks.title: Duplicate include guard

checks.shortDescription: Permanent loops (like "while(1) {..}") are typically meant to be non-terminating and should not be terminated by other means.
checks.title: Exit from permanent loop

checks.shortDescription: All loops should have a fixed upper bound; the counter should also be incremented along all paths within the loop.
checks.title: Unbounded loop

checks.shortDescription: Avoiding recursion allows tools and people to better analyze the program.
checks.title: Uses of recursion

checks.shortDescription: Dynamic memory allocation (using malloc() or calloc()) should be confined to the initialization routines of a program.
checks.title: Dynamic allocation after initialization

checks.shortDescription: Task synchronization shall not be performed through the use of task delays.
checks.title: Use of delay function

checks.shortDescription: Nested use of semaphores or locks should be avoided.
checks.title: Avoid nested semaphores

checks.shortDescription: The use of semaphores or locks to access shared data should be avoided.
checks.title: Avoid semaphores

checks.shortDescription: Where nested locks are inevitable, they should always be taken in the same order.
checks.title: Out-of-order locks

checks.shortDescription: Unlock operations shall always appear within the body of the same function that performs the matching lock operation.
checks.title: Unreleased lock

checks.shortDescription: Using the goto statement complicates function control flow and hinders program understanding.
checks.title: Use of goto

checks.shortDescription: Using the setjmp and longjmp functions complicates control flow and hinders program understanding.
checks.title: Use of setjmp or longjmp

checks.shortDescription: In an enumerator list, the = construct should not be used to explicitly initialize members other than the first, unless all items are explicitly initialized.
checks.title: Irregular enum initialization

checks.shortDescription: All "extern" declarations should be placed in a header file that is included in every file referring to the corresponding data object.
checks.title: "extern" declaration in source file

checks.shortDescription: Global variables that are not accessed outside their own file should be made static to promote information hiding.
checks.title: Global could be static

checks.shortDescription: Global and file-scope variables that are accessed by only one function should be scoped within that function.
checks.title: Variable scope too large

checks.shortDescription: A local variable or parameter that hides a global variable of the same name.
checks.title: Local variable hides global variable

checks.shortDescription: The return value of each non-void function call should be checked for error conditions, or cast to (void) if irrelevant.
checks.title: Unchecked return value

checks.shortDescription: Functions should check their arguments before their first use.
checks.title: Unchecked function argument

checks.shortDescription: Assertions should check dynamic properties of pre-/post-conditions and invariants.
checks.title: Constant assertion

checks.shortDescription: All functions of more than 10 lines should have at least one assertion.
checks.title: Long function without assertion

checks.shortDescription: Assertions should be defined as Boolean tests, meaning "assert(p != NULL)" rather than "assert(p)".
checks.title: Non-boolean assertion

checks.shortDescription: Assertions should not have side-effects -- they may be disabled completely, changing program behavior.
checks.title: Assertion with side effects

checks.shortDescription: Typedefs that indicate size and signedness should be used in place of the basic types.
checks.title: Use of basic integral type

checks.shortDescription: In compound expressions with multiple sub-expressions the intended order of evaluation shall be made explicit with parentheses.
checks.title: Implicit precedence in compound expression

checks.shortDescription: The evaluation of a Boolean expression shall have no side effects.
checks.title: Side effect in a Boolean expression

checks.shortDescription: The use of the preprocessor must be limited to inclusion of header files and simple macro definitions.
checks.title: Disallowed preprocessor use

checks.shortDescription: The use of conditional compilation directives must be kept to a minimum -- e.g.
checks.title: Conditional compilation

checks.shortDescription: Macros must expand to complete syntactic units -- "#define MY_IF if(" is not legal.
checks.title: Partial macro

checks.shortDescription: Macros are not allowed to use complex preprocessor features like variable argument lists and token pasting.
checks.title: Undisciplined macro

checks.shortDescription: Macros shall not be #define'd within a function or a block.
checks.title: Macro definition in block

checks.shortDescription: #undef shall not be used.
checks.title: Use of #undef

checks.shortDescription: All #else, #elif and #endif preprocessor directives shall reside in the same file as the #if or #ifdef directive to which they are related.
checks.title: Mismatched conditional compilation directive

checks.shortDescription: Putting more than one statement on a single line hinders program understanding.
checks.title: More than one statement per line

checks.shortDescription: There should be no more than one variable declaration per line.
checks.title: Multiple variable declarations on one line

checks.shortDescription: Function length should be limited to what can be printed on a single sheet of paper (60 lines).
checks.title: Function too long

checks.shortDescription: The declaration of an object should contain no more than two levels of indirection.
checks.title: Pointer indirection in declaration too high

checks.shortDescription: Statements should contain no more than two levels of dereferencing per object.
checks.title: Too many pointer dereferences in statement

checks.shortDescription: Pointer dereference operations should not be hidden in macro definitions.
checks.title: Pointer dereference hidden in macro

checks.shortDescription: Pointer indirection may not be hidden by typedefs -- "typedef int* IntPtr;" is not allowed.
checks.title: Hidden pointer indirection

checks.shortDescription: Non-constant pointers to functions should not be used.
checks.title: Use of non-constant function pointer

checks.shortDescription: Conversions shall not be performed between a pointer to a function and any type other than an integral type.
checks.title: Invalid function pointer conversion

checks.shortDescription: #include directives in a file shall only be preceded by other preprocessor directives or comments.
checks.title: Misplaced include

checks.shortDescription: Bit fields with integral types should have explicit signedness only.
checks.title: Ambiguously signed bit-field member

checks.shortDescription: Checking for overflow of integer addition by comparing against one of the arguments of the addition does not work when the result of the addition is automatically promoted to a...
checks.title: Bad check for overflow of integer addition

checks.shortDescription: Using "x % 2 == 1" to check whether x is odd does not work for negative numbers.
checks.title: Bad check for oddness

checks.shortDescription: Checking the sign of the result of a bitwise operation may yield unexpected results.
checks.title: Sign check of bitwise operation

checks.shortDescription: Using comparisons as operands of other comparisons is unusual in itself, and most readers will require parentheses to be sure of the precedence.
checks.title: Unclear comparison precedence

checks.shortDescription: If the same sub-expression is added to both sides of a comparison, and there is no possibility of overflow or rounding, then the sub-expression is redundant and could be removed.
checks.title: Comparison with canceling sub-expression

checks.shortDescription: Comparing results of floating-point computations with '==' or '!=' is likely to yield surprising results since floating-point computation does not follow the standard rules of...
checks.title: Equality test on floating-point values

checks.shortDescription: A multiplication result that is converted to a larger type can be a sign that the result can overflow the type converted from.
checks.title: Multiplication result converted to larger type

checks.shortDescription: When a comparison operation, such as x < y, always returns the same result, it means that the comparison is redundant and may mask a bug because a different check was intended.
checks.title: Comparison result is always the same

checks.shortDescription: Comparing a variable to itself always produces the same result, unless the purpose is to check for integer overflow or floating point NaN.
checks.title: Self comparison

checks.shortDescription: Testing for overflow by adding a value to a variable to see if it "wraps around" works only for unsigned integer values.
checks.title: Signed overflow check

checks.shortDescription: An unsigned value is always non-negative, even if it has been assigned a negative number, so the comparison is redundant and may mask a bug because a different check was intended.
checks.title: Unsigned comparison to zero

checks.shortDescription: A 'continue' statement only re-runs the loop if the loop-condition is true.
checks.title: Continue statement that does not continue

checks.shortDescription: Finds function calls where the size of an array being passed is smaller than the array size of the declared parameter.
checks.title: Array argument size mismatch

checks.shortDescription: An array with elements of a derived struct type is cast to a pointer to the base type of the struct.
checks.title: Upcast array used in pointer arithmetic

checks.shortDescription: Finds conversions from unsigned to signed.
checks.title: Conversion changes sign

checks.shortDescription: A bitfield is implicitly downcast to a smaller integer type.
checks.title: Implicit downcast from bitfield

checks.shortDescription: Finds function calls whose result type is a floating point type, and which are casted to an integral type.
checks.title: Lossy function result cast

checks.shortDescription: A pointer type is converted to a smaller integer type.
checks.title: Lossy pointer cast

checks.shortDescription: A constant value other than zero is converted to a pointer type.
checks.title: Non-zero value cast to pointer

checks.shortDescription: Passing a value that is not a string literal 'format' string to a printf-like function can lead to a mismatch between the number of arguments defined by the 'format' and the...
checks.title: Non-constant format string

checks.shortDescription: Using the return value from snprintf without proper checks can cause overflow.
checks.title: Potentially overflowing call to snprintf

checks.shortDescription: A printf-like function called with too many arguments will ignore the excess arguments and output less than might have been intended.
checks.title: Too many arguments to formatting function

checks.shortDescription: Calling a printf-like function with too few arguments can be a source of security issues.
checks.title: Too few arguments to formatting function

checks.shortDescription: Calling a printf-like function with the wrong type of arguments causes unpredictable behavior.
checks.title: Wrong type of arguments to formatting function

checks.shortDescription: A function is called, and the same operation is usually performed on the return value - for example, free, delete, close etc.
checks.title: Inconsistent operation on return value

checks.shortDescription: The result value of a function is often checked for nullness, but not always.
checks.title: Inconsistent nullness check

checks.shortDescription: Japanese era changes can lead to code behaving differently.
checks.title: Hard-coded Japanese era start date in call

checks.shortDescription: Japanese era changes can lead to code behaving differently.
checks.title: Hard-coded Japanese era start date in struct

checks.shortDescription: When an arithmetic operation modifies a date by a constant value of 365, it may be a sign that leap years are not taken into account.
checks.title: Arithmetic operation assumes 365 days per year

checks.shortDescription: A field that represents a year is being modified by an arithmetic operation, but no proper check for leap years can be detected afterwards.
checks.title: Year field changed using an arithmetic operation without checking for leap year

checks.shortDescription: When the return value of a fallible time conversion function is not checked for failure, its output parameters may contain invalid dates.
checks.title: Unchecked return value for time conversion function

checks.shortDescription: An array of 365 items typically indicates one entry per day of the year, but without considering leap years, which would be 366 days.
checks.title: Unsafe array for days of the year

checks.shortDescription: The '=' operator may have been used accidentally, where '==' was intended.
checks.title: Assignment where comparison was intended

checks.shortDescription: A Boolean value (i.e.
checks.title: Boolean value in bitwise operation

checks.shortDescription: The '==' operator may have been used accidentally, where '=' was intended, resulting in a useless test.
checks.title: Comparison where assignment was intended

checks.shortDescription: The address of a field (except the first) will never be NULL, so it is misleading, at best, to check for that case.
checks.title: Dubious NULL check

checks.shortDescription: A pure expression whose value is ignored is likely to be the result of a typo.
checks.title: Expression has no effect

checks.shortDescription: An if-statement with an empty then-branch and no else-branch may indicate that the code is incomplete.
checks.title: Futile conditional

checks.shortDescription: Usage of a logical-not (!) operator as an operand for a bit-wise operation.
checks.title: Incorrect 'not' operator usage

checks.shortDescription: When a logical expression can be easily simplified, there may be an opportunity to improve readability by doing so, or it may indicate that the code contains a typo.
checks.title: Logical expression could be simplified

checks.shortDescription: A switch statement over an enum type is missing a case for some enum constant and does not have a default case.
checks.title: Missing enum case in switch

checks.shortDescription: A short-circuiting logical operator is applied to what looks like a flag.
checks.title: Short-circuiting operator applied to flag

checks.shortDescription: The return value for strcpy, strncpy, or related string copy functions have no reserved return value to indicate an error.
checks.title: Use of string copy function in a condition

checks.shortDescription: A for-loop iteration expression goes backward with respect of the initialization statement and condition expression.
checks.title: Inconsistent direction of for loop

checks.shortDescription: Using alloca in a loop can lead to a stack overflow
checks.title: Call to alloca in a loop

checks.shortDescription: Using a string that may not be null terminated as an argument to a string function can result in buffer overflow or buffer over-read.
checks.title: Potential improper null termination

checks.shortDescription: Using the result of a network-to-host byte order function, such as ntohl, as an array bound or length value without checking it may result in buffer overflows or other...
checks.title: Untrusted network-to-host usage

checks.shortDescription: Highlights structs for which the amount of padding would increase when migrating from a 32-bit architecture to 64-bit.
checks.title: Padding increased in 64-bit migration

checks.shortDescription: When using a format specifier like %d ("int"), on a 32-bit architecture it's acceptable to pass a long since it's of the same size; when migrating to a 64-bit architecture this...
checks.title: Non-portable call to printf

checks.shortDescription: Highlights structs whose members are not laid out optimally, in the sense that by reordering them one could reduce the amount of internal padding on a 64-bit architecture.
checks.title: Suboptimal type definition

checks.shortDescription: Adding a value to a pointer to check if it overflows relies on undefined behavior and may lead to memory corruption.
checks.title: Pointer overflow check

checks.shortDescription: Using a library function that does not check buffer bounds requires the surrounding program to be very carefully written to avoid buffer overflows.
checks.title: Potential buffer overflow

checks.shortDescription: Returning the c_str of a locally allocated std::string could cause the program to crash or behave non-deterministically because the memory is deallocated when the std::string...
checks.title: Return c_str of local std::string

checks.shortDescription: A function returns a pointer to a stack-allocated region of memory.
checks.title: Returning stack-allocated memory

checks.shortDescription: Storing the address of a local variable in non-local memory can cause a dangling pointer bug if the address is used after the function returns.
checks.title: Local variable address stored in non-local memory

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'strncpy' with the size of the source buffer as the third argument may result in a buffer overflow.
checks.title: Possibly wrong buffer size in string copy

checks.shortDescription: Use of memset where the size argument is computed as the size of some non-struct type.
checks.title: Suspicious call to memset

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'strncat' with an incorrect size argument may result in a buffer overflow.
checks.title: Potentially unsafe call to strncat

checks.shortDescription: Taking 'sizeof' of an array parameter is often mistakenly thought to yield the size of the underlying array, but it always yields the machine pointer size.
checks.title: Suspicious 'sizeof' use

checks.shortDescription: Reading from a local variable that has not been assigned to will typically yield garbage.
checks.title: Potentially uninitialized local variable

checks.shortDescription: Using 'strcat' without checking the size of the source string may result in a buffer overflow
checks.title: Potentially unsafe use of strcat

checks.shortDescription: Accessing the stack-allocated memory of a function after it has returned can lead to memory corruption.
checks.title: Use of expired stack-address

checks.shortDescription: When a nested loop uses the same iteration variable as its outer loop, the behavior of the outer loop easily becomes difficult to understand as the inner loop will affect its...
checks.title: Nested loops with same variable

checks.shortDescription: A constructor in C++ cannot delegate part of the object initialization to another by calling it.
checks.title: Incorrect constructor delegation

checks.shortDescription: When a class and its derived class both define non-virtual destructors, the destructor of the derived class may not always be called.
checks.title: Non-virtual destructor

checks.shortDescription: All base classes with a virtual function should define a virtual destructor.
checks.title: Non-virtual destructor in base class

checks.shortDescription: Copy assignment operators should guard against self assignment; otherwise, self assignment is likely to cause memory corruption.
checks.title: Self assignment check

checks.shortDescription: Throwing an exception from a destructor may cause immediate program termination.
checks.title: Exception thrown in destructor

checks.shortDescription: A call to a pure virtual function using a 'this' pointer of an object that is under construction may lead to undefined behavior.
checks.title: Unsafe use of this in constructor

checks.shortDescription: Calling a virtual function from a constructor or destructor rarely has the intended effect.
checks.title: Virtual call in constructor or destructor

checks.shortDescription: Using the TLS or SSLv23 protocol from the boost::asio library, but not disabling deprecated protocols, or disabling minimum-recommended protocols.
checks.title: boost::asio TLS settings misconfiguration

checks.shortDescription: Using a deprecated hard-coded protocol using the boost::asio library.
checks.title: boost::asio use of deprecated hardcoded protocol

checks.shortDescription: Checking a pointer for nullness after dereferencing it is likely to be a sign that either the check can be removed, or it should be moved before the dereference.
checks.title: Redundant null check due to previous dereference

checks.shortDescription: A 'const' modifier on a function return type is useless and should be removed for clarity.
checks.title: Constant return type

checks.shortDescription: A 'const' modifier on a member function return type is useless.
checks.title: Constant return type on member

checks.shortDescription: The iteration variable of a nested loop should have a descriptive name: short names like i, j, or k can cause confusion except in very simple loops.
checks.title: Error-prone name of loop variable

checks.shortDescription: An implicitly declared function is assumed to take no arguments and return an integer.
checks.title: Implicit function declaration

checks.shortDescription: When the type of a function argument is not compatible with the type of the corresponding parameter, it may lead to unpredictable behavior.
checks.title: Call to a function with one or more incompatible arguments

checks.shortDescription: A function call is passing fewer arguments than the number of declared parameters of the function.
checks.title: Call to function with fewer arguments than declared parameters

checks.shortDescription: A function call to a function passed more arguments than there are declared parameters of the function.
checks.title: Call to function with extraneous arguments

checks.shortDescription: Loading from a variable in its own initializer may lead to undefined behavior.
checks.title: Variable used in its own initializer

checks.shortDescription: When null is passed to a function that is SAL-annotated to forbid this, undefined behavior may result.
checks.title: SAL requires non-null argument

checks.shortDescription: When a return value is discarded even though the SAL annotation requires inspecting it, a recoverable error may turn into a whole-program crash.
checks.title: SAL requires inspecting return value

checks.shortDescription: Annotations are different between declaration and definition.
checks.title: Inconsistent SAL annotation

checks.shortDescription: Using the goto statement complicates function control flow and hinders program understanding.
checks.title: Use of goto

checks.shortDescription: Using the setjmp and longjmp functions complicates control flow and hinders program understanding.
checks.title: Use of setjmp or longjmp

checks.shortDescription: Recursion makes the program call graph cyclic and hinders program understanding.
checks.title: Use of recursion

checks.shortDescription: All loops should have a fixed upper bound; the counter should also be incremented along all paths within the loop.
checks.title: Unbounded loop

checks.shortDescription: Permanent loops (like "while(1) {..}") are typically meant to be non-terminating and should not be terminated by other means.
checks.title: Exit from permanent loop

checks.shortDescription: Dynamic memory allocation (using malloc() or calloc()) should be confined to the initialization routines of a program.
checks.title: Dynamic allocation after initialization

checks.shortDescription: Function length should be limited to what can be printed on a single sheet of paper (60 logical lines).
checks.title: Function too long

checks.shortDescription: Putting more than one statement on a single line hinders program understanding.
checks.title: More than one statement per line

checks.shortDescription: Each function over 20 logical lines should have at least two assertions.
checks.title: Too few assertions

checks.shortDescription: When an assertion has side effects, disabling assertions will alter program behavior.
checks.title: Assertion with side effects

checks.shortDescription: Assertions should check dynamic properties of pre-/post-conditions and invariants.
checks.title: Constant assertion

checks.shortDescription: Assertions should be defined as Boolean tests, meaning "assert(p != NULL)" rather than "assert(p)".
checks.title: Non-Boolean assertion

checks.shortDescription: Global variables that are not accessed outside their own file could be made static to promote information hiding.
checks.title: Global could be static

checks.shortDescription: Global and file-scope variables that are accessed by only one function should be scoped within that function.
checks.title: Variable scope too large

checks.shortDescription: Functions should check their arguments before their first use.
checks.title: Unchecked function argument

checks.shortDescription: The return value of each non-void function call should be checked for error conditions.
checks.title: Unchecked return value

checks.shortDescription: The use of conditional compilation directives must be kept to a minimum -- e.g.
checks.title: Conditional compilation

checks.shortDescription: Macros must expand to complete syntactic units -- "#define MY_IF if(" is not legal.
checks.title: Partial macro

checks.shortDescription: The use of the preprocessor must be limited to inclusion of header files and simple macro definitions.
checks.title: Disallowed preprocessor use

checks.shortDescription: Macros are not allowed to use complex preprocessor features like variable argument lists and token pasting.
checks.title: Undisciplined macro

checks.shortDescription: Function pointers are not permitted -- they make it impossible for a tool to prove the absence of recursion.
checks.title: Function pointer call

checks.shortDescription: Pointer indirection may not be hidden by typedefs -- "typedef int* IntPtr;" is not allowed.
checks.title: Hidden pointer indirection

checks.shortDescription: No more than one level of pointer nesting/dereferencing should be used.
checks.title: Pointer nesting too high

checks.shortDescription: Using the `memset` function to clear private data in a variable that has no subsequent use can make information-leak vulnerabilities easier to exploit because the compiler can...
checks.title: Call to `memset` may be deleted

checks.shortDescription: Data provided remotely is used in this external API without sanitization, which could be a security risk.
checks.title: Untrusted data passed to external API

checks.shortDescription: Data provided remotely is used in this external API without sanitization, which could be a security risk.
checks.title: Untrusted data passed to external API

checks.shortDescription: Accessing paths influenced by users can allow an attacker to access unexpected resources.
checks.title: Uncontrolled data used in path expression

checks.shortDescription: Using user-supplied data in an OS command, without neutralizing special elements, can make code vulnerable to command injection.
checks.title: Uncontrolled data used in OS command

checks.shortDescription: Writing user input directly to a web page allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.
checks.title: CGI script vulnerable to cross-site scripting

checks.shortDescription: Including user-supplied data in a SQL query without neutralizing special elements can make code vulnerable to SQL Injection.
checks.title: Uncontrolled data in SQL query

checks.shortDescription: Using externally controlled strings in a process operation can allow an attacker to execute malicious commands.
checks.title: Uncontrolled process operation

checks.shortDescription: Incorrect use of a function that accesses a memory buffer may read or write data past the end of that buffer.
checks.title: Call to memory access function may overflow buffer

checks.shortDescription: Exceeding the size of a static array during write or access operations may result in a buffer overflow.
checks.title: Overrunning write

checks.shortDescription: Buffer write operations with a length parameter that does not match the size of the destination buffer may overflow.
checks.title: Badly bounded write

checks.shortDescription: Buffer write operations that do not control the length of data written may overflow.
checks.title: Potentially overrunning write

checks.shortDescription: Buffer write operations that do not control the length of data written may overflow when floating point inputs take extreme values.
checks.title: Potentially overrunning write with float to string conversion

checks.shortDescription: Buffer write operations that do not control the length of data written may overflow.
checks.title: Unbounded write

checks.shortDescription: Buffer write operations that do not control the length of data written may overflow
checks.title: Likely overrunning write

checks.shortDescription: Calling a variadic function without a sentinel value may result in a buffer overflow if the function expects a specific value to terminate the argument list.
checks.title: Unterminated variadic call

checks.shortDescription: Accessing an array without first checking that the index is within the bounds of the array can cause undefined behavior and can also be a security risk.
checks.title: Unclear validation of array index

checks.shortDescription: Allocating a buffer using 'malloc' without ensuring that there is always space for the entire string and a zero terminator can cause a buffer overrun.
checks.title: No space for zero terminator

checks.shortDescription: Using externally-controlled format strings in printf-style functions can lead to buffer overflows or data representation problems.
checks.title: Uncontrolled format string

checks.shortDescription: String operations on user-controlled strings can result in buffer overflow or buffer over-read.
checks.title: User-controlled data may not be null terminated

checks.shortDescription: Arithmetic operations on user-controlled data that is not validated can cause overflows.
checks.title: User-controlled data in arithmetic expression

checks.shortDescription: Arithmetic operations on uncontrolled data that is not validated can cause overflows.
checks.title: Uncontrolled data in arithmetic expression

checks.shortDescription: If a variable is assigned the maximum or minimum value for that variable's type and is then used in an arithmetic expression, this may result in an overflow.
checks.title: Use of extreme values in arithmetic expression

checks.shortDescription: Comparisons between types of different widths in a loop condition can cause the loop to behave unexpectedly.
checks.title: Comparison of narrow type with wide type in loop condition

checks.shortDescription: A user-controlled integer arithmetic expression that is not validated can cause overflows.
checks.title: Potential integer arithmetic overflow

checks.shortDescription: Allocating memory with a size controlled by an external user can result in arbitrary amounts of memory being allocated.
checks.title: Uncontrolled allocation size

checks.shortDescription: A subtraction with an unsigned result can never be negative.
checks.title: Unsigned difference expression compared to zero

checks.shortDescription: Dereferencing an out-of-bounds pointer is undefined behavior and may lead to security vulnerabilities.
checks.title: Invalid pointer dereference

checks.shortDescription: Casting an HRESULT to/from a Boolean type and then using it in a test expression will yield an incorrect result because success (S_OK) in HRESULT is indicated by a value of 0.
checks.title: Cast between HRESULT and a Boolean type

checks.shortDescription: Authentication by checking that the peer's address matches a known IP or web address is unsafe as it is vulnerable to spoofing attacks.
checks.title: Authentication bypass by spoofing

checks.shortDescription: Only accept SSL certificates that pass certificate verification.
checks.title: Certificate result conflation

checks.shortDescription: Always check the result of certificate verification after fetching an SSL certificate.
checks.title: Certificate not checked

checks.shortDescription: Storing sensitive information in cleartext can expose it to an attacker.
checks.title: Cleartext storage of sensitive information in buffer

checks.shortDescription: Storing sensitive information in cleartext can expose it to an attacker.
checks.title: Cleartext storage of sensitive information in file

checks.shortDescription: Transmitting sensitive information across a network in cleartext can expose it to an attacker.
checks.title: Cleartext transmission of sensitive information

checks.shortDescription: Storing sensitive information in a non-encrypted database can expose it to an attacker.
checks.title: Cleartext storage of sensitive information in an SQLite database

checks.shortDescription: Non-HTTPS connections can be intercepted by third parties.
checks.title: Failure to use HTTPS URLs

checks.shortDescription: Using cryptographic algorithms with too small a key size can allow an attacker to compromise security.
checks.title: Use of a cryptographic algorithm with insufficient key size

checks.shortDescription: Using broken or weak cryptographic algorithms can allow an attacker to compromise security.
checks.title: Use of a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm

checks.shortDescription: Using an old version of OpenSSL can allow remote attackers to retrieve portions of memory.
checks.title: Use of a version of OpenSSL with Heartbleed

checks.shortDescription: Separately checking the state of a file before operating on it may allow an attacker to modify the file between the two operations.
checks.title: Time-of-check time-of-use filesystem race condition

checks.shortDescription: Using an iterator owned by a container whose lifetime has expired may lead to unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Iterator to expired container

checks.shortDescription: If the value of a call to 'c_str' outlives the underlying object it may lead to unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Use of string after lifetime ends

checks.shortDescription: Referencing the contents of a unique pointer after the underlying object has expired may lead to unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Use of unique pointer after lifetime ends

checks.shortDescription: Calling a function of the CreateProcess* family of functions, where the path contains spaces, introduces a security vulnerability.
checks.title: NULL application name with an unquoted path in call to CreateProcess

checks.shortDescription: An initialization function is used to initialize a local variable, but the returned status code is not checked.
checks.title: Conditionally uninitialized variable

checks.shortDescription: Implicit scaling of pointer arithmetic expressions can cause buffer overflow conditions.
checks.title: Suspicious pointer scaling

checks.shortDescription: Implicit scaling of pointer arithmetic expressions can cause buffer overflow conditions.
checks.title: Suspicious pointer scaling to char

checks.shortDescription: Implicit scaling of pointer arithmetic expressions can cause buffer overflow conditions.
checks.title: Suspicious pointer scaling to void

checks.shortDescription: Explicitly scaled pointer arithmetic expressions can cause buffer overflow conditions if the offset is also implicitly scaled.
checks.title: Suspicious add with sizeof

checks.shortDescription: Exposing system data or debugging information helps a malicious user learn about the system and form an attack plan.
checks.title: Exposure of system data to an unauthorized control sphere

checks.shortDescription: Exposing sensitive system data helps a malicious user learn about the system and form an attack plan.
checks.title: Potential exposure of sensitive system data to an unauthorized control sphere

checks.shortDescription: Mixing up the failure conditions of 'operator new' and 'operator new(std::nothrow)' can result in unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Incorrect allocation-error handling

checks.shortDescription: Parsing user-controlled XML documents and allowing expansion of external entity references may lead to disclosure of confidential data or denial of service.
checks.title: XML external entity expansion

checks.shortDescription: Use of a standard library function that does not guard against buffer overflow.
checks.title: Use of dangerous function

checks.shortDescription: Using `cin` without specifying the length of the input may be dangerous.
checks.title: Dangerous use of 'cin'

checks.shortDescription: Use of a standard library function that is not thread-safe.
checks.title: Use of potentially dangerous function

checks.shortDescription: Casting a byte string to a wide-character string is likely to yield a string that is incorrectly terminated or aligned.
checks.title: Cast from char* to wchar_t*

checks.shortDescription: Creating a file that is world-writable can allow an attacker to write to the file.
checks.title: File created without restricting permissions

checks.shortDescription: Opening a file with the O_CREAT flag but without mode argument reads arbitrary bytes from the stack.
checks.title: File opened with O_CREAT flag but without mode argument

checks.shortDescription: Setting a DACL to NULL in a SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR will result in an unprotected object.
checks.title: Setting a DACL to NULL in a SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR

checks.shortDescription: Locking mutexes in different orders in different threads can cause deadlock.
checks.title: Cyclic lock order dependency

checks.shortDescription: Calling the lock method of a mutex twice in succession might cause a deadlock.
checks.title: Mutex locked twice

checks.shortDescription: A lock that is acquired one or more times without a matching number of unlocks may cause a deadlock.
checks.title: Lock may not be released

checks.shortDescription: Using untrusted inputs in a statement that makes a security decision makes code vulnerable to attack.
checks.title: Untrusted input for a condition

checks.shortDescription: A loop with an unsatisfiable exit condition could prevent the program from terminating, making it vulnerable to a denial of service attack.
checks.title: Infinite loop with unsatisfiable exit condition

checks.shortDescription: Casting a value to an incompatible type can lead to undefined behavior.
checks.title: Type confusion

checks.shortDescription: Any one function (or method) will contain no more than 200 logical source lines of code.
checks.sourceRelativePath: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 1.ql
checks.title: AV Rule 1

checks.shortDescription: There shall not be any self-modifying code.
checks.sourceRelativePath: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 2.ql
checks.title: AV Rule 2

checks.shortDescription: All functions shall have a cyclomatic complexity number of 20 or less.
checks.sourceRelativePath: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 3.ql
checks.title: AV Rule 3

checks.shortDescription: Trigraphs will not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 11

checks.shortDescription: Digraphs will not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 12

checks.shortDescription: Multi-byte characters and wide string literals will not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 13

checks.shortDescription: Literal suffixes shall use uppercase rather than lowercase letters.
checks.title: AV Rule 14

checks.shortDescription: Only those characters specified in the C++ basic source character set will be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 9

checks.shortDescription: The error indicator errno shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 17

checks.shortDescription: The macro offsetof, in library <stddef.h>, shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 18

checks.shortDescription: <locale.h> and the setlocale function shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 19

checks.shortDescription: The setjmp macro and the longjmp function shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 20

checks.shortDescription: The signal handling facilities of <signal.h> shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 21

checks.shortDescription: The input/output library <stdio.h> shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 22

checks.shortDescription: The library functions atof, atoi and atol from library <stdlib.h> shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 23

checks.shortDescription: The library functions abort, exit, getenv and system from library <stdlib.h> should not be used.
checks.title: Dangerous system functions

checks.shortDescription: The time handling functions of library <time.h> shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 25

checks.shortDescription: Only the #ifndef, #define, #endif and #include preprocessor directives shall be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 26

checks.shortDescription: The #ifndef, #define and #endif directives will be used to prevent multiple inclusions of the same header files.
checks.title: AV Rule 27

checks.shortDescription: The #ifndef and #endif directives will only be used as defined in AV Rule 27 to prevent multiple inclusions of the same header file.
checks.title: AV Rule 28

checks.shortDescription: The #define pre-processor directive shall not be used to create inline macros.
checks.title: AV Rule 29

checks.shortDescription: The #define pre-processor directive shall not be used to define constant values.
checks.title: AV Rule 30

checks.shortDescription: The #define directive will only be used as part of the technique to prevent multiple inclusions of the same header file.
checks.title: AV Rule 31

checks.shortDescription: The #include pre-processor directive should only be used to include header files.
checks.title: Include header files only

checks.shortDescription: The #include directive shall use the <filename.h> notation to include header files.
checks.title: AV Rule 33

checks.shortDescription: Header files should contain header guards (#defines to prevent the file from being included twice).
checks.title: Missing header guard

checks.shortDescription: Header files (*.h) will not contain non-const variable definitions or function definitions.
checks.title: AV Rule 39

checks.shortDescription: Every implementation file shall include the header files that uniquely define the inline functions, types, and templates used.
checks.title: AV Rule 40

checks.shortDescription: Source lines will be kept to a length of 120 characters or less.
checks.title: AV Rule 41

checks.shortDescription: Each expression-statement will be on a separate line.
checks.title: AV Rule 42

checks.shortDescription: Tabs should be avoided.
checks.title: AV Rule 43

checks.shortDescription: All indentations will be at least two spaces and be consistent within the same source file.
checks.title: AV Rule 44

checks.shortDescription: All words in an identifier will be separated by the underscore character.
checks.title: AV Rule 45

checks.shortDescription: User-specified identifiers (internal and external) will not rely on significance of more than 64 characters.
checks.title: AV Rule 46

checks.shortDescription: Identifiers will not begin with the underscore character.
checks.title: AV Rule 47

checks.shortDescription: Indentifiers will not differ by: (a) only case, (b) only underscores, (c) O vs 0 and D, (d) I vs 1 and l, (e) S vs 5, (f) Z vs 2, (g) n vs h
checks.title: AV Rule 48

checks.shortDescription: All acronyms in an identifier will be composed of uppercase letters.
checks.title: AV Rule 49

checks.shortDescription: The first word of the name of a class, structure, namespace, enumeration, or type created with typedef will begin with an uppercase letter.
checks.title: AV Rule 50

checks.shortDescription: All letters contained in function and variable names will be lowercase.
checks.title: AV Rule 51

checks.shortDescription: Identifiers for constant and enumerator values shall be lowercase.
checks.title: AV Rule 52

checks.shortDescription: The following character sequences shall not appear in header file names: ', \, /*, //, or ".
checks.title: AV Rule 53.1

checks.shortDescription: Header files will always have a file name extension of .h.
checks.title: AV Rule 53

checks.shortDescription: Implementation files will always have a file name extension of .cpp.
checks.title: AV Rule 54

checks.shortDescription: The public, protected, and private sections of a class will be declared in that order.
checks.title: AV Rule 57

checks.shortDescription: When declaring and defining functions with more than two parameters, the leading parenthesis and the first argument will be written on the same line as the function name.
checks.title: AV Rule 58

checks.shortDescription: The statements forming the body of an if, else if, else, while, do-while or for statement shall always be enclosed in braces, even if the braces form an empty block.
checks.title: AV Rule 59

checks.shortDescription: Braces which enclose a block will be placed in the same column, on separate lines directly before and after the block.
checks.title: AV Rule 60

checks.shortDescription: Braces which enclose a block will have nothing else on the line except comments (if necessary).
checks.title: AV Rule 61

checks.shortDescription: Spaces will not be used around '.' or '->', nor between unary operators and operands.
checks.title: AV Rule 63

checks.shortDescription: Unneeded implicitly generated member functions shall be explicitly disallowed.
checks.title: AV Rule 68

checks.shortDescription: A member function that does not affect the state of an object will be declared const.
checks.title: AV Rule 69

checks.shortDescription: A class will have friends only when a function or object requires access to the private elements of the class, but is unable to be a member of the class for logical or efficiency...
checks.title: AV Rule 70

checks.shortDescription: Virtual functions should not be invoked from a constructor or destructor of the same class.
checks.title: Virtual call from constructor or destructor

checks.shortDescription: Calls to an externally visible operation of an object, other than its constructors, shall not be allowed until the object has been fully initialized.
checks.title: AV Rule 71

checks.shortDescription: Unnecessary default constructors shall not be defined.
checks.title: AV Rule 73

checks.shortDescription: Initialization of nonstatic class members will be performed through the member initialization list rather than through assignment in the body of a constructor.
checks.title: AV Rule 74

checks.shortDescription: Members of the initialization list shall be listed in the order in which they are declared in the class.
checks.title: AV Rule 75

checks.shortDescription: A copy constructor and an assignment operator shall be declared for classes that contain pointers to data items or nontrivial destructors.
checks.title: AV Rule 76

checks.shortDescription: Constructors with default arguments should not be signature-compatible with a copy constructor when their default arguments are taken into account.
checks.title: Constructor with default arguments will be used as a copy constructor

checks.shortDescription: All base classes with a virtual function should define a virtual destructor.
checks.title: No virtual destructor

checks.shortDescription: All resources acquired by a class should be released by its destructor.
checks.title: Resource not released in destructor

checks.shortDescription: The assignment operator shall handle self-assignment correctly.
checks.title: AV Rule 81

checks.shortDescription: An assignment operator should return a reference to *this.
checks.title: Overloaded assignment does not return 'this'

checks.shortDescription: When two operators are opposites, both should be defined and one should be defined in terms of the other.
checks.title: Opposite operator definition

checks.shortDescription: A stateful virtual base shall be explicitly declared in each derived class that accesses it.
checks.title: AV Rule 88.1

checks.shortDescription: Multiple inheritance should only be used in the following restricted form: n interfaces plus m private implementations, plus at most one protected implementation.
checks.title: Undisciplined multiple inheritance

checks.shortDescription: A base class shall not be both virtual and non-virtual in the same hierarchy.
checks.title: Inconsistent virtual inheritance

checks.shortDescription: An inherited nonvirtual function shall not be redefined in a derived class.
checks.title: AV Rule 94

checks.shortDescription: An inherited default parameter shall never be redefined.
checks.title: Redefined default parameter

checks.shortDescription: Arrays shall not be treated polymorphically.
checks.title: AV Rule 96

checks.shortDescription: Neither operand of an equality operator (== or !=) shall be a pointer to a virtual member function.
checks.title: AV Rule 97.1

checks.shortDescription: Arrays should not be used in interfaces.
checks.title: No raw arrays in interfaces

checks.shortDescription: Namespaces will not be nested more than two levels deep
checks.title: AV Rule 99

checks.shortDescription: A template specialization shall be declared before its use.
checks.title: AV Rule 104

checks.shortDescription: Functions should always be declared at file scope.
checks.title: Function declared in block

checks.shortDescription: Functions with variable number of arguments shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 108

checks.shortDescription: Functions with more than 7 arguments will not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 110

checks.shortDescription: A function must not return a pointer or reference to a non-static local object.
checks.title: Return stack-allocated object

checks.shortDescription: Functions will have a single exit point.
checks.title: AV Rule 113

checks.shortDescription: All functions that are not void should return a value on every exit path.
checks.title: Missing return statement

checks.shortDescription: If a function returns error information, then that information will be tested.
checks.title: AV Rule 115

checks.shortDescription: Functions shall not call themselves, either directly or indirectly (i.e.
checks.title: AV Rule 119

checks.shortDescription: Only valid C++ style comments shall be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 126

checks.shortDescription: Code that is not used (commented out) shall be deleted.
checks.title: AV Rule 127

checks.shortDescription: Every source file will be documented with an introductory comment that provides information on the file name, its contents, and any program-required information (eg.
checks.title: AV Rule 133

checks.shortDescription: Identifiers in an inner scope should not use the same name as an identifier in an outer scope, and therefore hide that identifier.
checks.title: Hiding identifiers

checks.shortDescription: Identifiers shall not simultaneously have both internal and external linkage in the same translation unit.
checks.title: AV Rule 138

checks.shortDescription: External objects will not be declared in more than one file.
checks.title: AV Rule 139

checks.shortDescription: The register storage class specifier shall not be used.
checks.title: Register variables

checks.shortDescription: All variables shall be initialized before use.
checks.title: AV Rule 142

checks.shortDescription: Variables will not be introduced until they can be initialized with meaningful values.
checks.title: AV Rule 143

checks.shortDescription: In an enumerator list, the = construct should not be used to explicitly initialize members other than the first, unless all items are explicitly initialized.
checks.title: Irregular enum initialization

checks.shortDescription: The underlying bit representation of floating point numbers should not be used in any way by the programmer.
checks.title: Do not expose float representation

checks.shortDescription: Enumeration types should be used instead of integer types (and constants) to select from a limited series of choices.
checks.title: Use of integer where enum is preferred

checks.shortDescription: Octal constants (other than zero) shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 149

checks.shortDescription: Hexadecimal constants will be represented using all uppercase letters.
checks.title: AV Rule 150

checks.shortDescription: A string literal must not be modified, as the result is undefined.
checks.title: Constant string literals

checks.shortDescription: Numeric values in code will not be used; symbolic values will be used instead.
checks.title: AV Rule 151

checks.shortDescription: Multiple variable declarations shall not be allowed on the same line.
checks.title: AV Rule 152

checks.shortDescription: Unions shall not be used.
checks.sourceRelativePath: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 153.ql
checks.title: AV Rule 153

checks.shortDescription: Failing to explicitly assign bit fields to unsigned integer or enumeration types may result in unexpected sign extension or overflow.
checks.sourceRelativePath: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 154.ql
checks.title: Possible signed bit-field member

checks.shortDescription: Bit fields will not be used to pack data into a word for the sole purpose of saving space.
checks.sourceRelativePath: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 155.ql
checks.title: AV Rule 155

checks.shortDescription: All the members of a structure (or class) shall be named and shall only be accessed via their names.
checks.sourceRelativePath: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 156.ql
checks.title: AV Rule 156

checks.shortDescription: The right hand operand of a && or || operator shall not contain side effects.
checks.title: AV Rule 157

checks.shortDescription: The operands of a logical && or || operator shall be parenthesized if the operands contain binary operators.
checks.title: AV Rule 158

checks.shortDescription: Operators ||, &&, and unary & shall not be overloaded
checks.title: AV Rule 159

checks.shortDescription: An assignment expression shall be used only as the expression in an expression statement.
checks.title: AV Rule 160

checks.shortDescription: Signed and unsigned values shall not be mixed in arithmetic or comparison operations.
checks.title: AV Rule 162

checks.shortDescription: Unsigned arithmetic shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 163

checks.shortDescription: The right-hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the left-hand operand.
checks.title: AV Rule 164

checks.shortDescription: The unary minus operator should not be applied to unsigned expressions - cast the expression to a signed type to avoid unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Negation of unsigned value

checks.shortDescription: The sizeof operator should not be used on expressions that contain side effects.
checks.title: Sizeof with side effects

checks.shortDescription: The comma operator shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 168

checks.shortDescription: More than two levels of pointer indirection shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 170

checks.shortDescription: Relational operations shall not be applied to pointer types except where both operands are of the same type and point to or into the same object.
checks.title: AV Rule 171

checks.shortDescription: The address of an object with automatic storage shall not be assigned to an object which persists after the object has ceased to exist.
checks.title: AV Rule 173

checks.shortDescription: A pointer shall not be compared to NULL or be assigned NULL; use plain 0 instead.
checks.title: AV Rule 175

checks.shortDescription: A typedef will be used to simplify program syntax when declaring function pointers.
checks.title: AV Rule 176

checks.shortDescription: Down casting (casting from base to derived class) shall only be allowed through virtual functions.
checks.title: AV Rule 178

checks.shortDescription: A pointer to a virtual base class shall not be converted to a pointer to a derived class.
checks.title: AV Rule 179

checks.shortDescription: Implicit conversions that may result in a loss of information shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 180

checks.shortDescription: Redundant explicit casts will not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 181

checks.shortDescription: Type casting from any type to or from pointers shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 182

checks.shortDescription: Floating point numbers shall not be converted to integers, unless such a conversion is a specified algorithmic requirement or is necessary for a hardware interface.
checks.title: AV Rule 184

checks.shortDescription: C++ style casts (const_cast, reinterpret_cast, and static_cast) shall be used instead of the traditional C-style cast.
checks.title: AV Rule 185

checks.shortDescription: There shall be no unreachable code.
checks.title: AV Rule 186

checks.shortDescription: All non-null statements shall potentially have a side-effect.
checks.title: AV Rule 187

checks.shortDescription: Labels will not be used, except in switch statements.
checks.title: AV Rule 188

checks.shortDescription: The goto statement shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 189

checks.shortDescription: The continue statement shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 190

checks.shortDescription: The break statement shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 191

checks.shortDescription: All if, else if constructs will contain either a final else clause or a comment indicating why a final else clause is not necessary.
checks.title: AV Rule 192

checks.shortDescription: Every non-empty case clause in a switch statement shall be terminated with a break statement.
checks.title: AV Rule 193

checks.shortDescription: All switch statements that do not intend to test for every enumeration value shall contain a final default clause.
checks.title: AV Rule 194

checks.shortDescription: A switch expression will not represent a Boolean value.
checks.title: AV Rule 195

checks.shortDescription: Using a switch statement when there are fewer than two non-default cases leads to unclear code.
checks.title: No trivial switch statements

checks.shortDescription: Floating point variables should not be used as loop counters.
checks.title: Avoid floats in for loops

checks.shortDescription: The initialization statement in a for loop will only initialize a single loop parameter.
checks.title: AV Rule 198

checks.shortDescription: The increment expression in a for loop will only update a single loop parameter.
checks.title: AV Rule 199

checks.shortDescription: Null initialize or increment expressions in for loops will not be used; a while loop will be used instead.
checks.title: AV Rule 200

checks.shortDescription: Numeric variables being used within a for loop for iteration counting should not be modified in the body of the loop.
checks.title: For loop variable changed in body

checks.shortDescription: Floating point variables shall not be tested for exact equality or inequality.
checks.title: AV Rule 202

checks.shortDescription: The value of an expression shall be the same under any order of evaluation that the standard permits.
checks.title: AV Rule 204.1

checks.shortDescription: A single operation with side-effects shall only be used by itself, to the right of an assignment, in a condition, as the only argument with side-effects in a function call, as a...
checks.title: AV Rule 204

checks.shortDescription: The volatile keyword shall not be used unless directly interfacing with hardware.
checks.title: AV Rule 205

checks.shortDescription: Allocation/deallocation from/to the free store (heap) shall not occur after initialization.
checks.title: AV Rule 206

checks.shortDescription: Unencapsulated global data will be avoided.
checks.title: AV Rule 207

checks.shortDescription: C++ exceptions shall not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 208

checks.shortDescription: The basic types of int, short, long, float and double shall not be used, but specific-length equivalents should be typedef'd accordingly for each compiler, and these type names...
checks.title: AV Rule 209

checks.shortDescription: Algorithms shall not make assumptions concerning how data is represented in memory.
checks.title: AV Rule 210

checks.shortDescription: Underflow or overflow functioning shall not be depended on in any special way.
checks.title: AV Rule 212

checks.shortDescription: No dependence shall be placed on C++'s operator precedence rules, below arithmetic operators, in expressions.
checks.title: AV Rule 213

checks.shortDescription: Assuming that non-local static objects, in separate translation units, are initialized in a special order shall not be done.
checks.title: AV Rule 214

checks.shortDescription: Pointer arithmetic will not be used.
checks.title: AV Rule 215
